page: Amazon Web Services i4i.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8375C processor. This x86_64 architecture system features 16 virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The processor includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the instance bundles 3750 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, while network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 9 Gbps. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the i4i.4xlarge demonstrates solid performance across storage, memory, and compute workloads. The system achieved a PassMark CPU score of 20,679.31 and a database operations score of 7,299.53. Memory bandwidth testing shows a high read-only rate of 828.37 GB/sec for cached small blocks, which drops to 95.9 GB/sec for larger uncached blocks, alongside a large-block write speed of 57.32 GB/sec. Redis SET operations reached 15.48 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput was measured at 20.85 GB/sec. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the instance processed 1060.1 tokens per second for prompt processing on a small 135M model, though performance scales down to 32.33 tokens per second on a medium 7B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the i4i.4xlarge offers a high density of local NVMe SSD storage relative to its compute and memory resources, making it a viable option for I/O-intensive workloads without the overhead of external storage attachments. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance, though the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for large-scale deep learning. This hardware profile is highly suited for transactional databases, NoSQL databases, Redis caching layers, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance designed for high-throughput, I/O-intensive workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 vCPUs (8 physical cores) running at 3.5 GHz, 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a bundled 3750 GB NVMe SSD. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration. In benchmarks, the instance delivers a PassMark CPU score of 20,679.31, a database operations score of 7,299.53, and handles 15.48 million Redis SET operations per second. Its memory subsystem achieves a cached read bandwidth of 828.37 GB/sec. With a baseline network bandwidth of 9 Gbps and no GPU hardware, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy applications. It is best suited for transactional databases, NoSQL systems, caching layers, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3750 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3750 GB NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: A storage-optimized x86_64 platform featuring high-speed local NVMe SSDs and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 vCPUs and 128 GB DDR4 RAM.
Bundled 3750 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
Delivers 15.48 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.
Baseline network bandwidth of 9 Gbps via the Nitro hypervisor.
Optimized for transactional databases, NoSQL systems, and caching layers.
